tests.then: Export button shows lock icon and premium badge clearly visible
tests.when: They look at the export button
tests.given: A free plan user views the meal logging interface

tests.then: Tooltip displays 'Export requires premium plan - upgrade to export your data'
tests.given: A free plan user hovers over export button on desktop

tests.then: Lock icon and premium indicator are visible without requiring hover
tests.given: A mobile free plan user views export options

tests.then: Upgrade modal appears with pricing and feature explanation, not a blocking paywall
tests.given: A free plan user clicks the premium-indicated export button

tests.then: Export button appears normal without premium indicators or restrictions
tests.given: A premium plan user accesses the export functionality

success.metric: CSAT score for premium upgrade flows

decision: Redesign export button to clearly indicate premium requirement with lock icon and tooltip, implementing upgrade prompt after work completion rather than blocking paywall
scope_in: Lock icon overlay on export button for free plan users
Upgrade modal triggered after work completion but before export
Clear pricing and feature explanation in upgrade flow
rationale: Evidence shows the current export paywall creates user frustration (2.1/5 CSAT) and poor conversions (4.2% vs 12-18% benchmark) because it feels deceptive. Users are willing to pay when timing is right - after completing work but before export attempt. Visual indicators will reduce 'unexpected' complaints (34 tickets/90 days) while maintaining click-through for conversion optimization. This extends our meal logging MVP's monetization strategy with transparent premium feature presentation.
scope_out: New premium features beyond existing export
Complete UI redesign beyond premium indicators

edge_cases.handling: Show upgrade modal with clear pricing and feature benefits, not a blocking paywall
edge_cases.scenario: Free user clicks export button despite premium indicators

edge_cases.handling: Use persistent lock icon and premium badge visible without interaction
edge_cases.scenario: Mobile user cannot access hover tooltip

edge_cases.handling: Present upgrade prompt that acknowledges their work completion and emphasizes data export value
edge_cases.scenario: User completes extensive meal logging work before noticing export limitation

edge_cases.handling: Show condensed upgrade reminder with direct link to pricing page
edge_cases.scenario: User repeatedly clicks export after seeing upgrade modal

agent_brief: Modify the export button in the meal logging app to show premium status clearly. For free plan users: Add a lock icon overlay and 'Premium' badge to the export button. Create a tooltip component showing 'Export requires premium plan - upgrade to export your data' on hover (desktop) or tap (mobile). Style the button with reduced opacity or muted colors to indicate premium status while keeping it clickable. Ensure mobile users can see the lock icon without hover interactions. When free users click export, replace the current blocking paywall with an upgrade modal that highlights their completed meal logging work and explains export benefits with clear pricing. For premium users, show the normal export button without any restrictions or indicators. Use the existing Tailwind styling from the calorie tracking app for consistency. Implement user plan detection to conditionally render the appropriate export button state. Test both user flows thoroughly - free users should understand export is premium before investing time, premium users should have seamless export access.

evidence_refs.quote: I didn't realise the export was a paid feature. I just hit the button and got a paywall. Felt like a trap.
34 tickets tagged 'export_blocked'. Most common complaint: unexpected.
I would have paid if the upgrade had come at the right moment. The paywall felt aggressive.

risks.concern: Users might attempt to manipulate client-side premium indicators to access export without payment
risks.mitigation: Implement server-side plan verification for all export requests, never rely solely on client-side premium status checks

risks.concern: Premium feature UI might inadvertently reveal system architecture or pricing strategy details
risks.mitigation: Keep tooltip and modal text generic, avoid exposing internal plan codes or pricing logic in client-side code

security_abuse.summary: Premium feature indicators present minimal security risks as they are UI enhancements to existing export functionality. Primary concerns are around plan verification and preventing premium feature bypass.
